attractions.name: Oceanografic
attractions.description: Europe's largest aquarium — 45,000+ animals, 500+ species, dolphin shows
attractions.tips: Start at Arctic end (belugas), arrive 20 min early for dolphin show

attractions.name: Bioparc
attractions.description: Immersive African zoo — no visible cages, underwater hippo viewing
attractions.tips: Start with savanna. Don't miss underwater hippo window

attractions.name: Museu de les Ciencies
attractions.description: 3 floors of interactive science — Leonardo da Vinci exhibition, space, genetics
attractions.tips: Go weekday morning to avoid school groups

attractions.name: Valencia Cathedral + Miguelete Tower
attractions.description: Gothic/Baroque/Renaissance mix, claims to house the Holy Grail. 207 steps for panoramic views
attractions.tips: Free during mass hours

attractions.name: La Lonja de la Seda
attractions.description: 15th-century Gothic silk exchange — UNESCO World Heritage, stunning twisted columns

attractions.name: Mercado Central
attractions.description: One of Europe's largest Art Nouveau food markets — 300+ stalls

location.address: Plaça de la Ciutat de Bruges

attractions.tips: Go early 8-10am. Mon-Sat 7:30-15:00, closed Sun

attractions.name: Turia Gardens
attractions.description: Spain's largest urban park — 9 km in a drained riverbed, Gulliver Park inside

attractions.name: L'Almoina Archaeological Centre
attractions.description: 2,500 m² underground ruins spanning 2,000+ years — Roman, Visigoth, Islamic

attractions.name: Torres de Serranos
attractions.description: 14th-century city gate with great old town views

attractions.name: L'Hemisferic
attractions.description: Europe's largest IMAX screen and planetarium inside a giant eye-shaped building
attractions.tips: Pick a planetarium show over IMAX — better for adults. Check schedule at cac.es

attractions.name: L'Iber Museum of Toy Soldiers
attractions.description: World's largest collection of historical miniatures — 100,000+ figures in obsessively detailed dioramas
attractions.tips: Adults enjoy it more than kids. Tue-Sat 11-14 & 16-19, Sun 11-14

attractions.name: Museum of Illusions
attractions.description: 60+ optical illusion exhibits — holograms, impossible rooms, gravity-defying perspectives
attractions.tips: Fun lighthearted break from history. Daily 10-21

attractions.name: Prehistoric Caves Exhibition
attractions.description: Immersive recreation of French prehistoric cave galleries with projections and reconstructions — ending March 31

location.address: Museu de Prehistoria, C/ de la Corona, 36

attractions.tips: Check availability — exhibition ends March 31, 2026

attractions.name: Museo del Arroz
attractions.description: Rice cultivation and its role in Valencian cuisine — housed in a historic rice mill near the port
attractions.tips: Pair with paella lunch nearby. Free on Sundays

attractions.name: Museo Fallero
attractions.description: Every pardoned Fallas ninot since 1934 — nearly a century of Valencian artistry and satire
attractions.tips: Better post-festival when it's quiet. Pair with City of Arts and Sciences nearby

attractions.name: Torres de Quart
attractions.description: Western medieval gate with visible Napoleonic cannonball damage — less crowded than Serranos

attractions.name: City of Arts and Sciences (Exterior)
attractions.description: Calatrava's futuristic complex — free to walk around, spectacular at sunset reflected in water pools
attractions.tips: Best at sunset when the buildings reflect in the water pools

attractions.name: Plaza de la Virgen
attractions.description: Historic heart of old town — Roman forum site, Turia fountain, surrounded by Cathedral and Basilica

location.address: Plaça de la Mare de Deu

attractions.name: Plaza del Ayuntamiento
attractions.description: Valencia's grand central square — ornate City Hall, fountains, flower stalls

attractions.name: Museo de Bellas Artes
attractions.description: Spain's second-best art gallery — El Greco, Goya, Velazquez. Always free
attractions.tips: Worth a walk-through for the building and garden setting even if galleries aren't your thing

attractions.name: Centre del Carme (CCCC)
attractions.description: Contemporary art in a 13th-century convent — go for the beautiful medieval cloisters

attractions.name: Jardin Botanico
attractions.description: 16th-century botanical garden with tropical species, cacti, and greenhouses — peaceful retreat

attractions.name: Viveros Gardens
attractions.description: Large park with fountains next to Bellas Artes museum — free and relaxing

location.address: C/ de Sant Pius V, s/n

attractions.name: Mercado Colon
attractions.description: Restored Modernist building turned upscale food court — try horchata and fartons
attractions.tips: Must try horchata and fartons here

attractions.name: Mercado de Ruzafa
attractions.description: Local market in trendy Ruzafa — less touristy, explore street art and tapas bars nearby

attractions.name: El Hachazo (Axe Throwing)
attractions.description: Throw axes at targets — no experience needed, instructor-guided, includes a drink
attractions.tips: Book ahead — popular on evenings and weekends

attractions.name: Virtual Zone VR
attractions.description: Europe's largest VR arena — free-roam multiplayer games, not a booth experience
attractions.tips: Combine with Ruzafa tapas and street art

attractions.name: Gulliver Park
attractions.description: Giant Gulliver sculpture with slides and climbing — fun even for adults, inside Turia Gardens

attractions.name: Cines Babel
attractions.description: Independent cinema showing films in original language — vintage charm, curated programme
attractions.tips: Check programme at cinesbabel.com. Perfect for a rainy evening

attractions.name: flet
attractions.description: we sleep here

attractions.name: Atenea Sky Rooftop
attractions.description: 360-degree panoramic viewpoint from the 8th floor of the Ateneo Mercantil. Rooftop bar with Valencia's most romantic sunset views.

location.address: Plaza del Ayuntamiento, 18 (bar entrance: C/ Moratin, 12)

attractions.tips: Go around 7-7:30 PM in late March for golden hour. 8 EUR partially deductible from drinks. Cocktails 8-14 EUR.

attractions.name: Blackbird Cafe
attractions.description: Specialty coffee with Valencia's best pastries. The brioche cinnamon bun alone is worth the visit. Second location in old town.

location.address: Carrer de la Reina Na Maria, 7, Ruzafa

attractions.tips: Also "Little Blackbird" at Carrer de les Danses, 2 (old town).

attractions.name: Bluebell Coffee Co.
attractions.description: Ruzafa's pioneering specialty coffee roaster. Own-roasted beans, excellent brunch, homemade cakes. Cozy interior with charming patio.
attractions.tips: 3-5 EUR coffee, 8-14 EUR brunch.

attractions.name: Bodega La Rentaora
attractions.description: Old-school El Carmen tavern. Tiny wooden tables, cheap house wine, massive portions of authentic Valencian dishes. Time capsule.
attractions.tips: 8-15 EUR/person. Portions are huge — 2-3 tapas per person is plenty.

attractions.name: Carrer del Progres — Cabanyal Tiles
attractions.description: Densest concentration of colourful Art Nouveau ceramic tile facades. Each house different — blues, greens, yellows, florals. Open-air architectural museum.
attractions.tips: Tiles weren't just decorative — they protected from corrosive sea air. Also walk Carrer de Sant Pere and Carrer de la Reina.

attractions.name: Calle de los Colores
attractions.description: Forgotten alley turned open-air gallery. One mural spans 60+ metres. The epicentre of El Carmen's street art scene.
attractions.tips: Best in morning light.

attractions.name: Casa Fran
attractions.description: Beautifully designed specialty coffee shop near Central Market. Top Danish roaster beans. One of Valencia's most photogenic cafes.
attractions.tips: 3-5 EUR coffee, 8-12 EUR breakfast.

attractions.name: Casa de les Gargoles
attractions.description: 1917 house with gargoyles, blue/white/green tiles, columns, and floral borders. Extraordinary decorative ambition on a modest fisherman's house.

attractions.name: La Casa Judia (The Jewish House)
attractions.description: 1930 Modernista building blending Arabic, Egyptian, and Hebrew decorative styles on one facade. Star of David at the entrance. Most people miss it.
attractions.tips: Exterior only (private building).

attractions.name: Casa Montana
attractions.description: Historic bodega since 1836 with original wooden wine barrels. Exceptional wine selection, refined tapas. Most atmospheric tapas spot in Valencia.
attractions.tips: Nearly 200 years old. Pair with a Cabanyal neighbourhood walk. 12-20 EUR/person.

attractions.name: Casa Vani
attractions.description: Michelin Bib Gourmand tapas bar in El Carmen. Award-winning quality at neighbourhood prices. Tiny — reservations needed.
attractions.tips: 10-18 EUR/person. Book for dinner — very small.

attractions.name: Centre del Carme Cloisters
attractions.description: 700-year-old Gothic and Renaissance cloisters in a converted Carmelite convent. Peaceful orange tree courtyards alongside free contemporary art exhibitions.
attractions.tips: Always free. Go for the medieval cloisters, stay for the art.

attractions.name: Palacio de Dos Aguas (Courtyards)
attractions.description: Rococo palace courtyards with marble fountains and terracotta figures. Most tourists miss these entirely. Also houses the Ceramics Museum.
attractions.tips: Free Saturday afternoons and Sundays. The facade alone is worth seeing from outside.

attractions.name: Espai Verd
attractions.description: 1986 brutalist residential building where every balcony overflows with vegetation. A vertical garden decades before the concept was trendy.

location.address: Carrer del Music Hipolit Martinez, 16, Benimaclet

attractions.tips: Exterior only (private residence). 10-min tram ride to Benimaclet. Combine with Benimaclet neighbourhood walk.

attractions.name: Forn del Tossal
attractions.description: Historic bakery since 1795 on Plaza del Tossal. Traditional Valencian bread and pastries. 225+ years of continuous operation.
attractions.tips: 1-4 EUR per item. Perfect morning stop before exploring El Carmen street art.

attractions.name: GNOMO Design Shop
attractions.description: Design shop with a Wes-Anderson-worthy colourful 1980s facade. Curated postcards, stationery, ceramics. Perfect for unique souvenirs.
attractions.tips: Free to browse. The facade is Instagram-worthy.

attractions.name: Heladeria Llinares
attractions.description: Traditional Valencian ice cream since 1930, facing the Cathedral. Try leche merengada — uniquely Valencian frozen cinnamon-lemon drink.
attractions.tips: 3-5 EUR for 2 scoops. Almost 100 years old.

attractions.name: Heladeria Veneta
attractions.description: Ranked top 3 in the world at the Gelato Festival World Masters. Italian-owned, multiple international awards. Dutch chocolate is legendary.
attractions.tips: 3-5 EUR for 2 scoops. Best Ice Cream in Spain (2013, 2015), Best in Europe (2019).

attractions.name: Horchateria Daniel
attractions.description: Artisan horchata since 1949 in Alboraya — where tiger nuts actually grow. Third generation. Salvador Dali was a visitor. The real deal.
attractions.tips: Take tram Line 3/9 to Alboraya-Peris Arago (~15 min). Panach across the street is the main rival — try both.

attractions.name: Horchateria de Santa Catalina
attractions.description: Valencia's most iconic horchateria since 1830. Creamy chufa horchata with fartons. Steps from the Cathedral. Also churros con chocolate.
attractions.tips: ~5 EUR for horchata + fartons combo. Open daily 8:30-21:30.

attractions.name: Jardin de las Hesperides
attractions.description: Small mythological garden with citrus trees, classical sculptures, and irrigation canals. Greek legends meet Valencian orange heritage.

location.address: Carrer del Beat Gaspar Bono, s/n

attractions.tips: Tiny, literary, almost unknown to tourists. Near the Botanical Garden.

attractions.name: Jardin de Monforte
attractions.description: Hidden neoclassical garden with marble statues, fountains, and rose gardens behind high walls. One of the loveliest small gardens in Spain — almost always empty.
attractions.tips: Almost empty on weekday mornings. Time feels suspended.

attractions.name: Jardin de Polifilo
attractions.description: 40,000 m² garden inspired by a 1499 Renaissance novel. Rose island, decorative ponds, literary zones. Almost unknown — you'll likely have it to yourselves.

location.address: Near Palacio de Congresos, north of Turia

attractions.tips: 3 km from centre, worth it if in the area. Spring/summer 9:00-21:00.

attractions.name: La Pilareta (Bar Pilar)
attractions.description: Iconic tapas bar since 1917. Famous for clochinas al vapor. Chaotic, loud, 100+ years old, still packed with locals.
attractions.tips: Clochinas in season May-Aug but great year-round tapas. 6-12 EUR/person.

attractions.name: Carrer de Lope de Rueda & Pineda
attractions.description: El Carmen's most photogenic streets. Narrow lanes with galleries, vintage shops, street art, coloured facades, and hanging plants. Village feeling inside a city.

location.address: Between Plaza del Tossal and Torres de Serranos

attractions.name: Marina Beach Club
attractions.description: Year-round beachfront lounge at the marina. Pool, DJ sets, cocktails, Mediterranean vibes. Open from 17:30 even in March.

location.address: Carrer del Marina Real Juan Carlos I, 19

attractions.tips: Book ahead for Friday/Saturday. Cocktails 10-14 EUR.

attractions.name: Marina Real Juan Carlos I
attractions.description: Modern waterfront with harbour views, mega-yachts, restaurants, and the Veles e Vents building. Great for evening strolls and sunset drinks.
attractions.tips: Marina Beach Club open year-round (from 17:30 in March) — closest thing to a chiringuito in winter.

attractions.name: Masusa Paella Bar
attractions.description: Unfussy Ruzafa corner restaurant with laser focus on paella. Deeply flavorsome, genuinely local, very reasonable prices.
attractions.tips: 12-18 EUR/person. No pretense, just excellent rice. Lunch.

attractions.name: Deih Murals — Carrer de Na Jordana
attractions.description: Building-scale surreal murals by Valencian artist Deih. Futuristic, cosmic, human-alien landscapes. Internationally recognised art on his home turf.

location.address: Carrer de Na Jordana, El Carmen

attractions.name: Olhops Craft Beer
attractions.description: Ruzafa taproom with 20+ rotating taps. Tasting flights available. Casual, walk-in, great for craft beer lovers.
attractions.tips: 3-5 EUR/beer, tasting flight ~12-15 EUR. No booking needed.

attractions.name: Palacio de Cervello
attractions.description: 18th-century palace with original frescoes, period furniture. Napoleon stayed here. Free guided tours — even most locals don't know about it.
attractions.tips: Free guided tours — check schedule, book ahead. Houses the Municipal Archive.

attractions.name: PichiAvo Bacchus Mural
attractions.description: Massive mural fusing classical Greek mythology with graffiti wildstyle lettering. By Valencian duo PichiAvo in their home neighbourhood.
attractions.tips: PichiAvo exhibit internationally but this is their home turf.

attractions.name: Playa de la Malvarrosa
attractions.description: Valencia's iconic 1.8 km city beach. Palm-lined promenade, beachfront restaurants, and the stretch that inspired painter Sorolla.
attractions.tips: Casa Carmela nearby for legendary traditional paella (book ahead).

attractions.name: Playa de la Patacona
attractions.description: Locals' favourite beach, quieter than Malvarrosa. Beachfront restaurants in converted traditional houses. Best for a peaceful sunset walk.
attractions.tips: La Mas Bonita Patacona is the standout beachfront restaurant. Combine with horchata in Alboraya.

attractions.name: Plaza del Tossal Street Art
attractions.description: Crossroads of Valencia's urban art scene. Murals by Escif and Blu. Surrounding streets dense with pieces in every direction.
attractions.tips: Walk Carrer de Dalt, Carrer de Baix, Carrer de Lliria for densest murals.

attractions.name: Pont de l'Assut de l'Or (The Harp)
attractions.description: Calatrava's dramatic white cable-stayed bridge. 125m pylon is the highest point in Valencia. Best viewed from below with water reflections.

location.address: City of Arts and Sciences, eastern Turia

attractions.tips: Best at night when illuminated. Reflections in Agora pools are stunning.

attractions.name: Port Saplaya (Little Venice)
attractions.description: Colourful rainbow buildings along artificial canals with moored boats. Reminiscent of Venice and Burano. Genuinely photogenic and unique.
attractions.tips: ~8 km from centre. Bus L112A/L112B (40-60 min, 1.50 EUR) or taxi ~15 EUR. Restaurante Mosquetero has Menu del Dia ~16 EUR with views.

attractions.name: Portal de Valldigna
attractions.description: 1400 stone gateway in the 11th-century Moorish wall. Literally stepping through a door between two civilisations. Morning light through the arch is stunning.

location.address: Carrer del Portal de Valldigna / Carrer de Salines

attractions.tips: Morning light is best for photos.

attractions.name: Puente del Mar
attractions.description: 1592 Renaissance stone bridge with saint pavilions on the balustrades. Oldest and most beautiful bridge in Valencia. Glows warm at sunset.

location.address: Connects Paseo de la Alameda to Plaza America

attractions.tips: Evening light is best.

attractions.name: Puente de las Flores
attractions.description: Pedestrian bridge lined with 27,000 flowerpots. Late March spring plantings should be vibrant. Most photogenic bridge crossing in Valencia.

location.address: Crosses Turia between old town and Gran Via

attractions.tips: Spring blooms at their best in late March.

attractions.name: Restaurante Levante
attractions.description: Widely considered best traditional paella in Valencia city. Wood-fired, chicken, rabbit, local beans. Off the tourist path — where locals go.

location.address: Av. de Manuel de Falla, 12, Campanar

attractions.tips: Book a few days ahead. Lunch only (paella is a lunch dish). 15-25 EUR/person.

attractions.name: Tallat Coffee Roasters
attractions.description: Specialty coffee heaven near the beach. Extreme precision roasting — the nerd's choice. Combine with a Cabanyal walk.
attractions.tips: 3-5 EUR coffee.

attractions.name: Tasca Angel
attractions.description: Traditional Valencian tapas since 1946. Nothing over 7 EUR. Legendary fried sardines. True hole-in-the-wall behind the Silk Exchange.
attractions.tips: 5-12 EUR per person. Packed with regulars — arrive early or late.

attractions.name: Terraza 270
attractions.description: Stylish rooftop terrace at Barcelo Valencia Hotel. Best views of the City of Arts and Sciences at sunset. Cocktails 10-15 EUR.
attractions.tips: Perfect after an afternoon at the City of Arts and Sciences.

attractions.name: Ubik Cafe
attractions.description: Cafe-bookshop-gallery-music venue that helped spark Ruzafa's creative transformation. Unpretentious, slightly bohemian cultural heart.
attractions.tips: Free entry. Creative, unpretentious atmosphere.

attractions.name: Veles e Vents Building
attractions.description: Iconic modernist building with floating terraces overlooking the marina and Mediterranean. One of the best free sunset spots in Valencia.
attractions.tips: Nobody recommends this in tourist guides. Great sunset spot. Varadero bar inside for drinks.

day_trips.name: Sagunto Day Trip
day_trips.description: 2,500 years of layered history — Roman theatre (Spain's first National Monument), 1 km hilltop castle spanning seven civilisations, Jewish quarter. All attractions free.

sub_attractions.name: Roman Theatre (Teatro Romano)
sub_attractions.description: 1st-century AD Roman theatre carved into hillside. Spain's first National Monument. 4,000-seat capacity. Panoramic views over town and coastal plain.
sub_attractions.tips: Free. Tue-Sat 10:00-18:00, Sun 10:00-14:00. Closed Mon.

sub_attractions.name: Sagunto Castle
sub_attractions.description: 1 km fortress along a hilltop ridge with seven sections spanning 2,500 years. Roman Forum remains, Moorish walls, panoramic 360° views of sea, mountains, and orange groves.
sub_attractions.tips: Free. 15-20 min steep climb. Bring water. Best views in the region.

sub_attractions.name: Jewish Quarter (Juderia)
sub_attractions.description: Medieval Jewish quarter with narrow streets, one of the few maintained under Moorish rule

sub_attractions.name: Sagunto Jewish Quarter
sub_attractions.description: Compact maze of narrow medieval streets with whitewashed houses and ogival arches. One of the best-preserved Jewish quarters in the Valencia region.

location.address: Calle del Castillo / Portalet de la Juderia, Sagunto

sub_attractions.tips: Free. Enter through the Portalet de la Juderia on Calle del Castillo.

sub_attractions.name: Temple of Diana Remains
sub_attractions.description: 5th-4th century BC Iberian/Roman temple wall — one of the oldest standing structures in the Valencian Community. Survived Hannibal's 219 BC siege.
sub_attractions.tips: Free. Visible from the street, next to Church of Santa Maria.

day_trips.tips: All attractions free. Don't visit Monday (closed). Train 30 min, ~3.50 EUR each way.

day_trips.name: Albufera + El Palmar Day Trip
day_trips.description: Spain's largest freshwater lagoon surrounded by rice paddies. Birthplace of paella. 300+ bird species. Sunset boat rides and authentic paella in El Palmar village.

location.address: El Palmar, 15 km south of Valencia

sub_attractions.name: Albufera Boat Ride
sub_attractions.description: 45-min ride on traditional wooden boats through reed channels and the open lagoon. Sunset turns the water to gold and red. The #1 sunset experience near Valencia.
sub_attractions.tips: 5 EUR shared, 6 EUR sunset (Albumar.es). Arrive 30 min before sunset for best light.

sub_attractions.name: Authentic Paella Lunch in El Palmar
sub_attractions.description: Eat paella at its birthplace — El Palmar restaurants serve the real deal

sub_attractions.name: Albufera Nature Park Walk
sub_attractions.description: Walking trails through wetlands — birdwatching, rice fields, lagoon views

sub_attractions.name: El Palmar Village
sub_attractions.description: Tiny fishing village — birthplace of paella. Traditional barracas, atmospheric jetties, 20+ rice restaurants. Ground zero for authentic Valencian paella.
sub_attractions.tips: Arroceria Maribel (Michelin Bib Gourmand) and Bon Aire (international paella winner) are the best. Menu del dia ~18 EUR weekdays.

sub_attractions.name: Raco de l'Olla Visitor Centre
sub_attractions.description: Free interpretation centre with birdwatching hides and exhibits on Albufera's ecology. Late March is excellent for wading birds, flamingos, and glossy ibis.

location.address: Between Valencia and El Palmar (bus 24/25 stop nearby)

sub_attractions.tips: Free. Mon-Sun 9:00-14:00. Bring binoculars. Up to 100 species on a good March day.

sub_attractions.name: La Devesa (Dehesa del Saler)
sub_attractions.description: Wild dune beaches, pine forests, and Mediterranean scrub on the sandbar between the lagoon and sea. 6 walking trails. Valencia's only virgin beach.

location.address: Between El Saler and El Palmar

sub_attractions.tips: Free. The 4 km Scenic Trail is the most rewarding. Flat and accessible.

return_trip.notes: Check last bus time — can be early

day_trips.tips: Bus #24 from Porta de la Mar (1.50 EUR, 50 min). Boat ride 5 EUR/pp. Go afternoon for sunset. Paella is a lunch dish.

day_trips.name: Xativa Day Trip
day_trips.description: Double castle on a mountain ridge, birthplace of the Borgia popes. Fine Arts Museum with the famous upside-down Philip V portrait. Town of 1,000 fountains.

sub_attractions.name: Castell de Xativa
sub_attractions.description: Double castle on a mountain ridge — Castell Menor (Iberian/Roman) and Castell Major (Gothic/medieval). Prison cells, chapels, and sweeping views over the Costera valley.
sub_attractions.tips: 6 EUR combined ticket includes both museums. Book online — capacity limited. 25-30 min steep climb, do first when fresh.

sub_attractions.name: Xativa Old Town & Fountains
sub_attractions.description: Town of 1,000 fountains. Neoclassical Royal Fountain (25 jets), rare Gothic fountain, Borgia Palace, atmospheric cobbled streets, and lively market square.
sub_attractions.tips: Free to walk. Casa la Abuela for legendary arroz al horno (~17 EUR menu del dia).

sub_attractions.name: Museo de Bellas Artes de Xativa
sub_attractions.description: Fine art museum with the famous upside-down portrait of Philip V — the city's symbolic revenge for being burned in 1707. Works by Ribera and Goya.
sub_attractions.tips: Included in 6 EUR castle ticket. The upside-down Philip V is the unofficial symbol of Xativa.

sub_attractions.name: Ermita de Sant Feliu
sub_attractions.description: Oldest church in the Kingdom of Valencia. Romanesque doorway, recycled Roman columns, and the largest medieval Gothic murals surviving in the region.

location.address: Base of castle hill, on the road up

sub_attractions.tips: Free. On the path to the castle — stop on the way up. Closed Mon.

day_trips.tips: Train C-2 ~50-60 min, ~4-5 EUR each way. Book castle tickets online (capacity limited). Castle climb 25-30 min — do it first while fresh. Avoid Monday. Casa la Abuela for legendary arroz al horno.

day_trips.name: Gandia Day Trip
day_trips.description: Borgia Ducal Palace, old town, 3 km golden beach, Marjal wetlands for birdwatching. Birthplace of fideua — the noodle version of paella.

sub_attractions.name: Ducal Palace of the Borgias
sub_attractions.description: 14th-century Gothic-Renaissance palace of the Borgia dukes. Crown Hall, Golden Gallery, Chapel of Saint Francis Borgia. Audio guide in 7 languages.

location.address: Carrer Duc Alfons el Vell, 1, Gandia

sub_attractions.tips: Book online. Joint ticket with Monastery of Cotalba = 12 EUR (great value). Mon-Sat 10:00-14:00 / 15:00-19:00, Sun 10:00-14:00.

sub_attractions.name: MAGa Archaeological Museum
sub_attractions.description: Prehistory museum in a 14th-century hospital. Pieces from Cova del Parpalló — one of Europe's key Upper Paleolithic sites. Free on Sundays.
sub_attractions.tips: 2 EUR, free Sundays. The building itself is worth seeing.

sub_attractions.name: Gandia Beach & Marjal Wetlands
sub_attractions.description: 3 km golden beach with 7 km promenade. The Marjal wetlands (1,300 ha) between town and beach — boardwalks, flamingos, migratory birds. March is perfect.
sub_attractions.tips: Bus L2 from old town to beach (~20 min, 1.60 EUR). Eat fideua at one of the beachfront restaurants.

day_trips.tips: Train C1 ~1h15, ~5.80 EUR each way. Ducal Palace ~7-8 EUR (must-see). Skip Collegiate Church (10 EUR — too pricey). Bus L2 to beach for fideua lunch. Joint monastery+palace ticket 12 EUR if visiting Cotalba.

day_trips.name: Montanejos Hot Springs Day Trip
day_trips.description: Natural thermal springs at constant 25°C in a river gorge. Free access. Combine with the Ruta de los Estrechos gorge hike (8 km, 2.5-3h).

sub_attractions.name: Fuente de los Banos Hot Springs
sub_attractions.description: Crystal-clear turquoise thermal pools at constant 25°C year-round in a river gorge. Blue Flag certified. 6,000 litres/min geothermal spring. Free access.
sub_attractions.tips: Bring swimwear, towel, and water shoes (rocky riverbed). Water is 25°C even in March. Changing facilities may be closed off-season.

sub_attractions.name: Ruta de los Estrechos
sub_attractions.description: 8 km gorge hike through dramatic narrows with wooden walkways and suspension bridges over turquoise water. One of the best moderate hikes in the Valencia region.
sub_attractions.tips: 2.5-3 hours, moderate difficulty. Wear proper shoes. Best done in morning, then hot springs in afternoon.

day_trips.tips: Limited bus (Mon/Thu only, ~10 EUR each way). Organized tours 60-85 EUR/pp. Bring swimwear, towel, and water shoes (rocky riverbed). Water is 25°C even in March.

day_trips.name: Peniscola Day Trip
day_trips.description: Medieval walled town on a rocky headland jutting into the sea. Papa Luna Castle, Game of Thrones filming location. Dramatic, photogenic, worth the long journey.

sub_attractions.name: Papa Luna Castle
sub_attractions.description: 13th-century Templar fortress, home of antipope Benedict XIII. Dramatic position on a rocky headland. Game of Thrones filming location (Meereen, Season 6).
sub_attractions.tips: 5 EUR. Winter hours 10:30-17:30. The views from the battlements are spectacular.

sub_attractions.name: Peniscola Old Town
sub_attractions.description: Walled medieval streets climbing to the castle. Stone archways, whitewashed houses, sea views from every turn. El Bufador sea blowhole at the base.
sub_attractions.tips: Free to walk. El Bufador (blowhole) is best at high tide. 2-3 hours for a thorough explore.

day_trips.tips: HIFE bus recommended (~15-25 EUR each way, 2.5h, 7/day). Castle 5 EUR. Longest day trip — needs early start. March: artichokes in season in nearby Benicarlo.

day_trips.name: Requena Day Trip
day_trips.description: Medieval walled town with underground wine caves, Gothic churches, silk museum, and the Utiel-Requena wine region. Authentically lived-in, not polished for tourists.

sub_attractions.name: Cuevas de la Villa
sub_attractions.description: 22 interconnected underground chambers beneath the medieval quarter. Ossuary, granary, wine cellars with grape-stomping pools. 1.2 km route at constant 15-16°C.

location.address: Plaza de Albornoz, 6, La Villa, Requena

sub_attractions.tips: 4 EUR or combined ticket with 4 museums for 8 EUR. Wed-Sun 10:30-14:00 & 16:00-19:00. Closed Mon/Tue.

sub_attractions.name: La Villa (Requena Medieval Quarter)
sub_attractions.description: Walled medieval quarter with cobbled streets, stone mansions, Jewish Quarter, Moorish Alcazaba ruins. Authentically quiet — feels like stepping back 500 years.
sub_attractions.tips: Free to walk. Enter through medieval gates.

sub_attractions.name: Iglesia del Salvador
sub_attractions.description: Outstanding Isabelline Gothic church (1380-1533). Richly sculpted facade with floral chains, virgins, and angels. Stunning octagonal Communion Chapel with blue-tiled dome.

location.address: La Villa, adjacent to Cuevas entrance

sub_attractions.tips: Check locally for visiting hours. Adjacent to the caves.

sub_attractions.name: Museo del Vino
sub_attractions.description: Wine heritage museum in the 15th-century Palacio del Cid. History of Utiel-Requena D.O. and the native Bobal grape. 2,500 years of winemaking.
sub_attractions.tips: 2 EUR or included in combined ticket (8 EUR with caves + 3 other museums).

sub_attractions.name: Casa del Arte Mayor de la Seda
sub_attractions.description: Three floors of silk heritage in the 1740 Guild of Silk Weavers HQ. Requena's surprising connection to Valencia's silk trade.
sub_attractions.tips: Included in combined ticket.

day_trips.tips: Train C-3 ~75-85 min, ~5-7 EUR each way. Caves 4 EUR, combined ticket with 4 museums just 8 EUR. Much colder than Valencia (700 m altitude) — bring layers. Avoid Mon/Tue (closed).

day_trips.name: Sierra Calderona Day Trip
day_trips.description: Mountain hiking 45 min from the city. Moorish castle ruins, pine forests, panoramic views from 600 m peaks. 7+ trails for all levels. Free entry.

sub_attractions.name: Castell de Serra Hike
sub_attractions.description: 7 km circular hike to 8th-century Moorish castle ruins at 536 m. Square tower, crenellated walls, panoramic Turia valley views. El Cid conquered this castle in 1095.

location.address: Start: Serra Town Hall (Plaça del Poble)

sub_attractions.tips: Moderate difficulty, 207 m elevation gain. Best all-round trail. Combine with village lunch.

sub_attractions.name: Garbi Viewpoint Hike
sub_attractions.description: 10-12 km hike to 601 m summit. Best panorama in the park — Mediterranean, Valencia skyline, Albufera lagoon, and interior mountain ranges visible.

location.address: Start: Serra town or Area Recreativa del Garbi

sub_attractions.tips: Moderate to demanding, ~400 m elevation gain. Late March is ideal — brutally hot in summer. Bring 1.5L water.

sub_attractions.name: Serra Village
sub_attractions.description: Authentic mountain village at the foot of Sierra Calderona. Sculpture Route, Oil Museum, natural springs. White houses, orange trees, zero tourists.
sub_attractions.tips: Casa Granero for a sit-down lunch. Bar Domingo on the square for post-hike beers.

day_trips.tips: Metro L1 to Betera + bus 230 to Serra. Check bus schedule — limited! Taxi from Betera ~12-18 EUR if bus doesn't work. Castell de Serra trail (7 km, 2h) is the best moderate option.

groupings.name: City Center
groupings.description: Historic old town attractions within walking distance

groupings.name: City of Arts & Sciences
groupings.description: The modern architectural complex in Turia riverbed
